matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an HFE iron-homeostasis thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, iron overload susceptibility, or hereditary hemochromatosis review. Penetrance and clinical meaning depend on genotype combination and iron studies.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs1800562 (HFE C282Y) dosage suggests this sample carries the HFE prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests an HFE iron-homeostasis thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, iron overload susceptibility, or hereditary hemochromatosis review. Penetrance and clinical meaning depend on genotype combination and iron studies. This specific marker is curated as HFE C282Y iron-overload marker. rs1800562 (HFE C282Y) is curated as a HFE marker for Hereditary hemochromatosis review context, Iron overload and hepcidin regulation. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the iron sensing, transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, and systemic iron homeostasis pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs1800562 (HFE C282Y) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the HFE prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag iron sensing, transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, and systemic iron homeostasis.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an HFE iron-homeostasis thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, iron overload susceptibility, or hereditary hemochromatosis review. Penetrance and clinical meaning depend on genotype combination and iron studies. An observed HFE variant places this sample in the curated HFE research context. The local HFE bundle is hereditary hemochromatosis and iron-metabolism oriented. Penetrance is incomplete and interpretation depends on genotype combination, sex, ferritin/transferrin saturation, ancestry, and clinical context. C282Y and H63D are common HFE markers with genotype-combination-dependent hemochromatosis relevance.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Transferrin receptor and iron homeostasis biology

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a HFE variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests an HFE iron-homeostasis thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, iron overload susceptibility, or hereditary hemochromatosis review. Penetrance and clinical meaning depend on genotype combination and iron studies. HFE methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a iron sensing, transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, and systemic iron homeostasis gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local HFE bundle is hereditary hemochromatosis and iron-metabolism oriented. Penetrance is incomplete and interpretation depends on genotype combination, sex, ferritin/transferrin saturation, ancestry, and clinical context. C282Y and H63D are common HFE markers with genotype-combination-dependent hemochromatosis relevance.
